purpose: Stylesheet loaded by the WebView preview document (via https://markdownplusplus.local/preview.css inside the WebView, or inlined when exporting HTML standalone). Provides a GitHub-like Markdown body style with light + dark schemes, a generous trailing pad (::after height = calc(100vh - 48px)) so the last line can scroll to the top of the viewport for scroll-sync accuracy, table/code/blockquote rules, and a .mermaid + .mermaid-error skin. @media print strips the trailing pad and max-width for clean printed/PDF output.
exports: .markdown-body h1 / h2 / h3 / a / blockquote / pre / code / pre code / table / th / td / tr:nth-child(2n) / input[type=checkbox]
invariants: The trailing .markdown-body::after spacer is required for scroll-sync to be able to position the last paragraph at the top of the viewport - removing it breaks bottom-of-document scroll alignment.
Dark-mode is triggered by prefers-color-scheme, not by an in-app toggle; preview.js mirrors this when initializing Mermaid's theme.
gotchas: The CSP set in MarkdownRenderer allows style-src https://markdownplusplus.local 'unsafe-inline'; only the linked preview.css is loaded over the local virtual host - inline <style> blocks from rendered markdown would be permitted but cmark-gfm tagfilter strips them.
